sourceMovie.title: Dead Silence
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Dead Silence for the same reason it broke through in 2007: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. James Wan keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dead Silence clicked for you, Resident Evil (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dead Silence?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil, Van Helsing, 28 Weeks Later—each shares DNA with Dead Silence while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dead Silence?
faq.answer: Dead Silence (2007) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dead Silence worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
